# Vibe Skeptic Showcase

## Project Overview

This showcase is a simple single page application that will showcase all projects created on the VibeSkeptic github account.

Use the GitHub api to fetch all repos and display them in a friendly way that shows the project names/descriptions/github stats (stars/forks/etc) and then a link to their homepage if applicable.

## Tech Stack

- **Backend**: None
- **Frontend**: React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 8, Mantine 8, PNPM

## References

Mantine: https://mantine.dev/llms.txt

## Common Commands

When setting up the project set it up with the following commands:
`pnpm start` - Spin up the vite dev server
`pnpm build` - Build the site for production
`pnpm test` - Run any tests

## Best Practices

### README.md
Create and maintain the README as the project is being developed.

### Git
Before making a commit, make sure the project builds without error and all tests are passing.

At the end of every prompt, create a commit with the following commit message structure:

> claude: <insert commit message>
> More description if needed

**Key Mantine 8 Components for Your GitHub Showcase:**

**Layout & Structure**
- `Container`: Responsive wrapper with max-width, use this as your page container
- `Grid`: Simple flexbox-based grid system with `Grid.Col` for responsive columns
- `Stack`: Vertical layout primitive (replaces old Group for vertical stacking)
- `Group`: Horizontal layout for small related elements

**Card Components**
- `Card`: Perfect for repo items with `Card.Section` for dividing content areas
- `Paper`: Simpler alternative for custom card styling
- Both support padding, radius, shadow, and hover effects

**Typography**
- `Title`: Use for repo names with size variants (h1, h2, h3)
- `Text`: Body text for descriptions, with truncation support
- `Anchor`: Links to repo homepages/GitHub URLs

**Visual Elements**
- `Badge`: Display stats (stars, forks) as colorful badges
- `Group`: Arrange badges horizontally

**Theming**
- Import `MantineProvider` to wrap your app
- Configure `theme` prop with colors, spacing, typography
- Use `useStyles` hook or `sx` prop for component-level styling

**Practical Structure Example:**
```
Container
  Grid (responsive)
    Grid.Col (per repo)
      Card
        Card.Section (repo header)
          Title (repo name)
        Text (description)
        Group (badges for stars/forks)
        Anchor (homepage link)
```

For complete Mantine 8 API details, visit https://mantine.dev/ directly in your browser, or let me know if you'd like help implementing a specific component.

import {
  Card,
  Text,
  Title,
  Badge,
  Group,
  Anchor,
  Stack,
  ActionIcon,
  Tooltip,
} from '@mantine/core'
import {
  IconStar,
  IconGitFork,
  IconAlertCircle,
  IconExternalLink,
  IconBrandGithub,
} from '@tabler/icons-react'
import type { Repo } from '../types'

interface RepoCardProps {
  repo: Repo
}

const LANGUAGE_COLORS: Record<string, string> = {
  TypeScript: 'blue',
  JavaScript: 'yellow',
  Python: 'green',
  Rust: 'orange',
  Go: 'cyan',
  Ruby: 'red',
  Java: 'grape',
  'C#': 'violet',
  'C++': 'pink',
  Shell: 'gray',
}

export function RepoCard({ repo }: RepoCardProps) {
  const langColor = repo.language ? (LANGUAGE_COLORS[repo.language] ?? 'gray') : undefined

  return (
    <Card shadow="sm" padding="lg" radius="md" withBorder h="100%" style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
      <Stack gap="sm" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
        <Group justify="space-between" align="flex-start" wrap="nowrap">
          <Title order={3} size="h4" style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}>
            {repo.name}
          </Title>
          <Group gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
            {repo.homepage && (
              <Tooltip label="Visit homepage">
                <ActionIcon
                  component="a"
                  href={repo.homepage}
                  target="_blank"
                  rel="noopener noreferrer"
                  variant="subtle"
                  color="blue"
                  aria-label="Homepage"
                >
                  <IconExternalLink size={16} />
                </ActionIcon>
              </Tooltip>
            )}
            <Tooltip label="View on GitHub">
              <ActionIcon
                component="a"
                href={repo.html_url}
                target="_blank"
                rel="noopener noreferrer"
                variant="subtle"
                color="gray"
                aria-label="GitHub"
              >
                <IconBrandGithub size={16} />
              </ActionIcon>
            </Tooltip>
          </Group>
        </Group>

        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
          {repo.description ?? 'No description provided.'}
        </Text>

        {repo.topics.length > 0 && (
          <Group gap={4}>
            {repo.topics.slice(0, 5).map((topic) => (
              <Badge key={topic} size="xs" variant="light" color="blue">
                {topic}
              </Badge>
            ))}
          </Group>
        )}

        <Group gap="xs" mt="auto">
          {repo.language && (
            <Badge size="sm" variant="dot" color={langColor}>
              {repo.language}
            </Badge>
          )}
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconStar size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.stargazers_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconGitFork size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.forks_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          {repo.open_issues_count > 0 && (
            <Group gap={4}>
              <IconAlertCircle size={14} />
              <Text size="xs">{repo.open_issues_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
            </Group>
          )}
        </Group>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  )
}

input.content:
# Vibe Skeptic Showcase

A single-page application that showcases all public, non-forked repositories from the [vibe-skeptic](https://github.com/vibe-skeptic) GitHub account.

## Features

- Fetches repos from the GitHub API at runtime (no build-time secrets required)
- Displays project name, description, language, topics, stars, forks, and open issues
- Live search/filter across names, descriptions, and topics
- Links to each project's GitHub page and homepage (when available)
- Responsive grid layout — works great on mobile, tablet, and desktop

## Tech Stack

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| React | 19 |
| TypeScript | 5.9 |
| Vite | 6 |
| Mantine UI | 8 |
| PNPM | latest |

## Getting Started

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm start      # dev server at http://localhost:5173
pnpm build      # production build → dist/
pnpm test       # run tests
```

## Notes

The app calls the public GitHub API (`https://api.github.com/orgs/vibe-skeptic/repos`). Unauthenticated requests are limited to 60 per hour per IP. For higher throughput you can set `VITE_GITHUB_TOKEN` and update the fetch headers accordingly.

import {
  Card,
  Text,
  Title,
  Badge,
  Group,
  Anchor,
  Stack,
  ActionIcon,
  Tooltip,
} from '@mantine/core'
import {
  IconStar,
  IconGitFork,
  IconAlertCircle,
  IconExternalLink,
  IconBrandGithub,
} from '@tabler/icons-react'
import type { Repo } from '../types'

interface RepoCardProps {
  repo: Repo
}

const LANGUAGE_COLORS: Record<string, string> = {
  TypeScript: 'blue',
  JavaScript: 'yellow',
  Python: 'green',
  Rust: 'orange',
  Go: 'cyan',
  Ruby: 'red',
  Java: 'grape',
  'C#': 'violet',
  'C++': 'pink',
  Shell: 'gray',
}

export function RepoCard({ repo }: RepoCardProps) {
  const langColor = repo.language ? (LANGUAGE_COLORS[repo.language] ?? 'gray') : undefined

  return (
    <Card shadow="sm" padding="lg" radius="md" withBorder h="100%" style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
      <Stack gap="sm" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
        <Group justify="space-between" align="flex-start" wrap="nowrap">
          <Title order={3} size="h4" style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}>
            {repo.name}
          </Title>
          <Group gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
            {repo.homepage && (
              <Tooltip label="Visit homepage">
                <ActionIcon
                  component="a"
                  href={repo.homepage}
                  target="_blank"
                  rel="noopener noreferrer"
                  variant="subtle"
                  color="blue"
                  aria-label="Homepage"
                >
                  <IconExternalLink size={16} />
                </ActionIcon>
              </Tooltip>
            )}
            <Tooltip label="View on GitHub">
              <ActionIcon
                component="a"
                href={repo.html_url}
                target="_blank"
                rel="noopener noreferrer"
                variant="subtle"
                color="gray"
                aria-label="GitHub"
              >
                <IconBrandGithub size={16} />
              </ActionIcon>
            </Tooltip>
          </Group>
        </Group>

        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
          {repo.description ?? 'No description provided.'}
        </Text>

        {repo.topics.length > 0 && (
          <Group gap={4}>
            {repo.topics.slice(0, 5).map((topic) => (
              <Badge key={topic} size="xs" variant="light" color="blue">
                {topic}
              </Badge>
            ))}
          </Group>
        )}

        <Group gap="xs" mt="auto">
          {repo.language && (
            <Badge size="sm" variant="dot" color={langColor}>
              {repo.language}
            </Badge>
          )}
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconStar size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.stargazers_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconGitFork size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.forks_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          {repo.open_issues_count > 0 && (
            <Group gap={4}>
              <IconAlertCircle size={14} />
              <Text size="xs">{repo.open_issues_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
            </Group>
          )}
        </Group>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  )
}

    19→export default function App() {

message.content: I would like to link a substack article for some of the projects and have that show up as a link in the cards. How would you recommend we go about doing that? Where should I put the link so that it can be retrieved in the api for you to use?

content.text:
A few options, with trade-offs:

---

**Option 1: Static config file in this repo (recommended)**

Add a `src/substack.json` file mapping repo name → article URL:

```json
{
  "my-repo-name": "https://vibeskeptic.substack.com/p/some-article"
}
```

Pros: no extra network requests, easy to maintain, lives alongside the code, you control the format.
Cons: requires a code deploy to update.

---

**Option 2: GitHub repo's "Website" field**

Set the homepage field on each GitHub repo to the Substack article URL instead of a project homepage.

Pros: zero config files, all data in one place.
Cons: can't have *both* a project homepage and a Substack link, and it conflates two different concepts.

---

**Option 3: GitHub repo topics**

Add a specially-prefixed topic like `substack-p-article-slug` to each repo.

Pros: no extra files or fetches.
Cons: topics have strict character limits/restrictions (no colons or slashes), so you'd need a lookup from slug → full URL — more fragile.

---

**My recommendation: Option 1.** It's the most flexible — you can add/remove links without touching GitHub, support both a homepage *and* a Substack link per project, and the file is easy to read and edit. The only cost is a redeploy when you add a new link, which is a small price.

Want me to implement that?

message.content: I would like to do it without an additional deploy and would like support for both a website and article. Topics may work, but are there other ways to go about doing this as well?

content.text:
Good constraint. A few no-redeploy options that support both a homepage and a Substack link:

---

**Option A: Public GitHub Gist**

Maintain a single public gist with a JSON mapping (`repo-name → substack-url`). The app fetches it at runtime via the GitHub API. To add a new link, just edit the gist — no code change, no deploy.

Pros: dead simple to update, version-controlled with history, no extra repos.
Cons: one extra network request on load, gist ID is hardcoded in the app (one-time).

---

**Option B: Raw JSON file in a GitHub repo**

Keep a `substack.json` (or similar) in a public repo (could be a dedicated `vibeskeptic/data` repo, or even the showcase repo itself on `main`). Fetch it via `raw.githubusercontent.com` at runtime. Updating the file is just a commit — no deploy triggered.

Pros: familiar git workflow, works if you already have a data/content repo.
Cons: same extra network request; raw GitHub URLs have caching (up to 5 min delay on updates).

---

**Option C: GitHub repo topics with a prefix convention**

Add a topic like `substack-article-slug` to each repo. The app strips the prefix and constructs the full URL (e.g. `https://vibeskeptic.substack.com/p/{slug}`). Already in the API response — zero extra fetches.

Pros: no extra files or requests, purely data-driven from GitHub.
Cons: only works if your Substack article slugs are simple enough to fit in a topic (lowercase, hyphens only, ≤50 chars). Can't encode an arbitrary URL.

---

**My recommendation: Option A (Gist)** if your article URLs are arbitrary, or **Option C (topics)** if your Substack slugs are simple and short. The gist approach is the most flexible and still requires zero app deploys to maintain.

Want me to implement one of these?

import {
  Card,
  Text,
  Title,
  Badge,
  Group,
  Stack,
  ActionIcon,
  Tooltip,
} from '@mantine/core'
import {
  IconStar,
  IconGitFork,
  IconAlertCircle,
  IconExternalLink,
  IconBrandGithub,
} from '@tabler/icons-react'
import type { Repo } from '../types'

interface RepoCardProps {
  repo: Repo
}

const LANGUAGE_COLORS: Record<string, string> = {
  TypeScript: 'blue',
  JavaScript: 'yellow',
  Python: 'green',
  Rust: 'orange',
  Go: 'cyan',
  Ruby: 'red',
  Java: 'grape',
  'C#': 'violet',
  'C++': 'pink',
  Shell: 'gray',
}

export function RepoCard({ repo }: RepoCardProps) {
  const langColor = repo.language ? (LANGUAGE_COLORS[repo.language] ?? 'gray') : undefined

  return (
    <Card shadow="sm" padding="lg" radius="md" withBorder h="100%" style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column' }}>
      <Stack gap="sm" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
        <Group justify="space-between" align="flex-start" wrap="nowrap">
          <Title order={3} size="h4" style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}>
            {repo.name}
          </Title>
          <Group gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
            {repo.homepage && (
              <Tooltip label="Visit homepage">
                <ActionIcon
                  component="a"
                  href={repo.homepage}
                  target="_blank"
                  rel="noopener noreferrer"
                  variant="subtle"
                  color="blue"
                  aria-label="Homepage"
                >
                  <IconExternalLink size={16} />
                </ActionIcon>
              </Tooltip>
            )}
            <Tooltip label="View on GitHub">
              <ActionIcon
                component="a"
                href={repo.html_url}
                target="_blank"
                rel="noopener noreferrer"
                variant="subtle"
                color="gray"
                aria-label="GitHub"
              >
                <IconBrandGithub size={16} />
              </ActionIcon>
            </Tooltip>
          </Group>
        </Group>

        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed" style={{ flex: 1 }}>
          {repo.description ?? 'No description provided.'}
        </Text>

        {repo.topics.length > 0 && (
          <Group gap={4}>
            {repo.topics.slice(0, 5).map((topic) => (
              <Badge key={topic} size="xs" variant="light" color="blue">
                {topic}
              </Badge>
            ))}
          </Group>
        )}

        <Group gap="xs" mt="auto">
          {repo.language && (
            <Badge size="sm" variant="dot" color={langColor}>
              {repo.language}
            </Badge>
          )}
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconStar size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.stargazers_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconGitFork size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.forks_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
          </Group>
          {repo.open_issues_count > 0 && (
            <Group gap={4}>
              <IconAlertCircle size={14} />
              <Text size="xs">{repo.open_issues_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>
            </Group>
          )}
        </Group>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  )
}

  Card,
  Text,
  Title,
  Badge,
  Group,
  Stack,
  ActionIcon,
  Tooltip,
} from '@mantine/core'
import {
  IconStar,
  IconGitFork,
  IconAlertCircle,
  IconExternalLink,
  IconBrandGithub,
  IconNews,
} from '@tabler/icons-react'
import type { Repo } from '../types'

interface RepoCardProps {
  repo: Repo
}

const LANGUAGE_COLORS: Record<string, string> = {
  TypeScript: 'blue',
  JavaScript: 'yellow',
  Python: 'green',
  Rust: 'orange',
  Go: 'cyan',
  Ruby: 'red',
  Java: 'grape',
  'C#': 'violet',
  'C++': 'pink',
  Shell: 'gray',
}

const SUBSTACK_PREFIX = 'substack-'
const SUBSTACK_BASE = 'https://vibeskeptic.substack.com/p/'

export function RepoCard({ repo }: RepoCardProps) {
  const langColor = repo.language ? (LANGUAGE_COLORS[repo.language] ?? 'gray') : undefined
  const substackSlug = repo.topics.find((t) => t.startsWith(SUBSTACK_PREFIX))?.slice(SUBSTACK_PREFIX.length)
  const substackUrl = substackSlug ? `${SUBSTACK_BASE}${substackSlug}` : null
  const visibleTopics = repo.topics.filter((t) => !t.startsWith(SUBSTACK_PREFIX))

  return (

          </Group>
        </Group>

        <Text size="sm" c="dimmed" style={{ flex: 1 }}>

        {repo.topics.length > 0 && (
          <Group gap={4}>
            {repo.topics.slice(0, 5).map((topic) => (
              <Badge key={topic} size="xs" variant="light" color="blue">
                {topic}
              </Badge>
            ))}
          </Group>
        )}

        <Group gap="xs" mt="auto">
          {repo.language && (
            <Badge size="sm" variant="dot" color={langColor}>
              {repo.language}
            </Badge>
          )}
          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconStar size={14} />
            <Text size="xs">{repo.stargazers_count.toLocaleString()}</Text>

          <Group gap={4}>
            <IconGitFork size={14} />

          {repo.open_issues_count > 0 && (
            <Group gap={4}>

}

content.text:
Done. To link a Substack article to a repo, just add a topic to that repo on GitHub following this pattern:

```
substack-{article-slug}
```

For example, if your article URL is `https://vibeskeptic.substack.com/p/my-cool-project`, add the topic `substack-my-cool-project`. The orange newspaper icon will appear on the card automatically — no deploy needed.
